workflow_name: Customer Escalation Email -> Risk Brief + Manager Loop
user_message: Give me the manager view on this Nod Publishers escalation email. Not urgent but would be good to have by EOD.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Samantha Singh <samantha.singh@nodpublisher.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-05 13:30 EST
Subject: Re: migration architecture Discussion - Next Steps

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with migration architecture. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Samantha Singh <samantha.singh@nodpublisher.com>
Date: 2024-02-26 14:20 EST
Subject: Follow-up: migration architecture Review

There's been a change in our timeline for migration architecture. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Samantha Singh <samantha.singh@nodpublisher.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-05 10:11 EST
Subject: URGENT: migration architecture Issue at Nod Publishers

Just checking in on the migration architecture proposal. Our VP Engineering is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF34-M trap (Strict separation of manager brief from customer draft when one unified response); produces complete Severity assessment, manager risk brief, customer-facing draft response, stakeholder map delta, fore
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF34-M failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF34-M failure mode (Strict separation of manager brief from customer draft when one unified response)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct migration architecture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF34-M -- Strict separation of manager brief from customer draft when one unified response
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
